summary: Gruber pays tribute to John Moltz's Crazy Apple Rumors Site (CARS), which is going on hiatus after six years. He highlights his favorite piece of CARS media criticism about Daring Fireball itself, and tells a story about how Moltz helped him get a Macworld Expo press credential through Phil Schiller, who was apparently a huge CARS fan. Gruber notes that CARS's fake rumors were often more accurate than serious Mac rumor sites. The piece is a characteristically understated farewell to a fellow member of the Apple blogosphere.
key_points: CARS is going on hiatus after six years of satirizing Apple rumors and the Mac community
Phil Schiller was a genuine fan of CARS, and Moltz leveraged that connection to help Gruber get a Macworld press credential
CARS's fake rumors were paradoxically more accurate than many serious Mac rumor sites
Gruber's favorite CARS piece was one that critiqued Daring Fireball itself
money_quotes: I, of course, complained to friends, self-important prick that I am.
A surprising amount of stuff in CARS that sounds made up is completely true, including this one.
So Moltz told me he'd "put in a good word" for me. I have no idea what that means exactly, but next thing I knew, I had a press credential for Macworld Expo.
Moltz's fake rumors were generally more accurate that most of those from the ostensibly serious Mac rumor sites.
All of which is to say that perhaps, in some way, we're maybe going to sort of miss CARS. I guess.
key_insight: The best Apple satire was so well-informed that its fake rumors were more accurate than the serious ones, and its creator had more insider access than many credentialed journalists.
